mnemonics: SRSM: Simple Random Samples Matter. (A reminder of the gold standard for avoiding bias).
BIAS: Be Informed About Sampling. (Highlights that bias stems from the sampling method, not the data itself).
CLT: Central Limit Theorem - 'The mean of the sample means is the population mean' (A core concept for sampling distributions).
keyPoints: Random sampling ensures every member of the population has an equal chance of selection, which helps to avoid selection bias.
The sampling distribution of a statistic (like the mean) is the distribution of that statistic, calculated from all possible samples of the same size from the population.
Bias is a systematic error introduced by the sampling method, not the randomness of who ended up in a single sample. It means your estimator is consistently wrong.
commonMistakes: Confusing the data distribution (shape of the population data) with the sampling distribution (distribution of a statistic like the sample mean).
Assuming that a large sample size will correct for a biased sampling method. A larger biased sample only gives you more precisely wrong results.
Believing that random sampling guarantees a representative sample for every single draw; its power is evident over many possible samples, not in a single instance.
